common.footerPrivacy: Your answers and results are processed entirely within your browser on this device. Nothing is sent to a server or external service.

home.heroTitle: Is your team ready to embrace AI?
home.heroSubtitle: Visualize your development process, team operations, and governance. A practical assessment to move your AI adoption to the next stage.
home.outcomesTitle: What you'll get from this assessment
home.outcomesSubtitle: We turn the often-vague notion of "AI adoption maturity" into something that leads directly to your next action.
home.outcome1: Build a shared understanding across your team of where AI adoption stands today
home.outcome2: Clarify which development processes to address next
home.outcome3: Take away improvement actions you can act on right away
home.flowTitle: Three steps to your next move
home.step1Body: Choose the assessment course that fits your team's current situation and answer the questions.
home.step2Body: Visualize your development process, governance, and AI adoption foundation from multiple angles.
home.step3Body: Use your score and recommended actions to prioritize improvements.
home.ctaTitle: Start with where you are today.
home.ctaSubtitle: Your results are saved in your browser so you can revisit them later.

assessmentSelect.title: Choose an assessment course
assessmentSelect.subtitle: Choose from two courses based on your team's situation. Both help you organize what to address next based on your results.
assessmentSelect.resumeTitle: You can resume your previous assessment
assessmentSelect.resumeBody: Your partial answers have been saved in this browser.
assessmentSelect.quickDescription: For teams that want a quick read on where they stand. A compact check of the fundamentals of development and AI adoption.
assessmentSelect.standardDescription: For a more detailed picture of your team's strengths and challenges. Get insights that lead directly to action.
assessmentSelect.footerSaved: Results are saved in your browser
assessmentSelect.footerConversation: A starting point for team conversations

assessment.noStorageWarning: This environment can't save your progress or history. Please don't close this screen while answering.
assessment.autoSaveNotice: Your answers are automatically saved in your browser
assessment.keyboardHint: You can also select an option using the 1–5 keys on your keyboard.

result.emptyBody: Once you complete the assessment, your team's current standing and next actions will appear here.
result.title: Assessment Result
result.subtitle: Knowing where you stand makes it easier to choose your next move. Start with the highest-priority improvements.
result.reductionLabel: Potential development productivity gain from AI adoption
result.guidanceAlert: This result is a guideline to help your team prioritize improvements. Use it alongside your actual development context to choose a reasonable next step.
result.planningPhaseWarning: Since this project is still in the planning/discussion stage, this result is a reference only. We recommend reassessing once your development approach is finalized.
result.unknownAlert: Some questions were left unanswered. For a more accurate result, check with your team and reassess.
result.nextActionTitle: Turn this result into your team's next conversation.
result.nextActionSubtitle: Share it, and start with the highest-priority actions.

resultSections.priorityActionsEmpty: There are currently no high-priority improvement items.
resultSections.processFitSubtitle: Rather than whether to use AI, this shows which process to start with and what guardrails to apply.
resultSections.processGroupRecommendedDescription: Processes where you can try AI on a small scale as-is and check the results.
resultSections.processGroupConditionalTitle: Use once conditions are in place
resultSections.processGroupConditionalDescription: Processes where you can safely expand AI adoption once rules and reviews are established.
resultSections.processGroupNotRecommendedDescription: Processes where building a solid development foundation should take priority over AI adoption.
resultSections.processEmptyBody: No processes apply to this category. No action is needed for this classification.
resultSections.codeReviewNote: A human must always give final review to AI review results
resultSections.documentationNote: AI-generated documentation should be reviewed by a subject-matter expert
resultSections.releaseNote: Final approval must always be given by a human
resultSections.operationsNote: Follow your confidential information handling rules when passing logs or error details to AI
resultSections.designNote: Without a record of design decisions, it's difficult to make suggestions that account for past decisions

design.name: Design
design.example: Comparing design options and identifying review points

code_review.name: Code Review
code_review.example: Summarizing changes and assisting with review

testing.name: Testing
testing.example: Assisting with test case and test perspective creation

operations.name: Operations
operations.example: Summarizing logs and assisting with initial incident triage

answers.emptyBody: Please access this page again from the results screen.
answers.title: All Answers
answers.subtitle: Every question you answered, along with an explanation for each.
answers.unknownComment: You answered "Not sure." Start by reviewing your current situation.

history.title: Assessment History
history.subtitle: Review your past results and track how your team has changed.
history.dataManagementBody: You can delete just your history, or all assessment data saved in this browser.
history.emptyBody: Once you complete your first assessment, you can track how your results change over time here.

about.title: Know where you stand to move AI-driven development forward.
about.intro: This assessment is designed to help you review the development foundation needed for AI adoption. Don't judge by the score alone — use it as a starting point for team conversations about what to tackle next.
about.axis1Description: Whether specifications and the rationale behind decisions are organized in a form the team can use
about.axis2Description: Whether planning, review, and release workflows are functioning well
about.axis3Description: Whether you have mechanisms in place to sustain testing and quality checks
about.axis4Description: Whether you're set up to use AI safely and reproducibly
about.axis5Description: Whether the nature of your work and your team's assumptions are clearly defined
about.infoScoreBody: Your answers are aggregated by area to place your team's maturity into a defined stage.
about.infoLimitBody: The result is not the single right answer. Use it in conjunction with your own on-the-ground context.
about.infoDataBody: In principle, your answers and results are saved only within this browser.
about.ctaTitle: Turn your team's conversation into action.
about.ctaBody: After the assessment, we recommend discussing "where to start" together as a team while reviewing the results.

share.shareXImageHint: Image copied. Paste it into the post composer.
share.clipboardImageAndTextSuccess: Copied the post text and image. Paste them into your post and attach the image
share.clipboardImageFailure: Your browser doesn't support copying images. Only the post text was copied

1.name: Level 1: Foundational work is needed before AI adoption
1.summary: Start by building the foundation for development. AI can serve as a partner for learning and organizing information.

2.name: Level 2: Limited AI assistance is possible
2.summary: You can use AI assistance in low-risk areas.

3.name: Level 3: Human-led AI development is possible
3.summary: You can use AI for practical tasks under human oversight.

4.name: Level 4: Well-suited for AI-driven development
4.summary: With your review and quality assurance practices in place, you can delegate a wide range of development tasks to AI.

5.name: Level 5: Well-suited for AI agent adoption
5.summary: You're positioned for continuous AI-driven development backed by quality gates.
uses: Continuous AI-driven development with quality gates

P1.text: What is the size of the development team this assessment covers?

P2.text: Is this development in a domain with especially strict regulatory or security requirements, such as finance, healthcare, or the public sector?

Q01.text: What type of development is this?
Q01.strengthText: Has a clear outlook for AI utilization suited to the development target

options.existing_feature_addition: Adding features to an existing system

Q02.text: What is the main development target?
Q02.strengthText: The development target has high affinity with AI assistance

Q03.text: Are the technologies used common ones that are still widely used today?

Q04.text: Are there multiple people who understand the system specifications?
Q04.strengthText: Understanding of specifications is shared among multiple people

options.partial_area: Multiple people understand only some areas

Q05.text: Is the project's README and environment setup procedure well maintained?
Q05.strengthText: The README and environment setup procedures are well maintained
Q05.description: Project overview, how to start the application, environment variables, development procedures, testing methods, etc.

Q06.text: Are requirements and specifications organized somewhere other than chat or verbal communication?
Q06.strengthText: Requirements and specifications are organized in a centralized location

options.organized: Organized in a wiki, document management tool, tickets, etc.
options.scattered: Mainly scattered across Excel, email, and chat

Q07.text: Are the reasons behind important design decisions and technology choices documented?
Q07.strengthText: The reasons for design decisions and technology choices are documented
Q07.description: This refers to a state where the reasons behind decisions can be reviewed later, not limited to formats such as ADRs (Architecture Decision Records).

Q08.text: Do you have coding guidelines or naming conventions?

Q09.text: Are API specifications and external system integration specifications organized?
Q09.strengthText: API and external integration specifications are organized

Q10.text: Is the database design and the meaning of data fields documented?
Q10.strengthText: The database design and data definitions are documented

Q11.text: Are business rules and exception handling documented?
Q11.strengthText: Business rules and exception handling are documented

Q12.text: Is there a place where you can review past incidents, precautions, and known issues?
Q12.strengthText: Past incidents and precautions have been turned into knowledge

Q13.text: Do you manage source code with Git?
Q13.strengthText: Version control with Git is well established

options.partial_git: Only part is managed with Git
options.other_tools: Managed with something other than Git, or via file sharing

Q14.text: Are there rules for Git commit messages?
Q14.strengthText: Commit message conventions are well established
Q14.description: For example, the purpose of the change is clear, ticket numbers are included, and the change unit size is appropriate

Q15.text: Do you conduct reviews via Pull Requests or Merge Requests?
Q15.strengthText: Review operations via Pull Requests are well established

Q16.text: Do you use a ticket management tool?
Q16.strengthText: Ticket management is well established in the development process
Q16.description: GitHub Issues, Jira, Backlog, Redmine, Azure DevOps, Linear, etc.

Q17.text: Do tickets include the purpose, requirements, and completion criteria?
Q17.strengthText: Tickets include the purpose, requirements, and completion criteria

Q18.text: Can development tasks be broken down into units of a few hours to a few days?
Q18.strengthText: Development tasks are broken down into an appropriate granularity

Q19.text: Are the procedures for development, review, testing, and release defined?
Q19.strengthText: The procedures from development through release are defined

Q20.text: Do you have a CI/CD or automated build system?
Q20.strengthText: CI/CD or automated builds are in place

Q33.text: Is it standard practice to commit frequently in small units, and to use rollbacks/reverts when problems occur?
Q33.strengthText: Small commits and rollback practices are well established
Q33.description: For example, committing work in small units, and reverting to a previous state or using revert when problems occur

options.multiple_types: Multiple types exist: unit, integration, and E2E

Q22.text: Are the checks to be performed after a change clearly defined?
Q22.strengthText: The checks to be performed after a change are clearly defined

Q23.text: Do you have code review standards?

Q24.text: Do you automatically run static analysis, linting, and type checking?
Q24.strengthText: Static analysis, linting, and type checking are automated

Q25.text: Is there human approval before deploying to production?
Q25.strengthText: Human approval before production deployment is well established

Q26.text: Do you have rules for handling confidential and personal information?
Q26.strengthText: Rules for handling confidential and personal information are in place

Q27.text: Do you use AI coding tools in your development work?
Q27.strengthText: AI coding tools are used on an ongoing basis

Q28.text: Are the types of information that may or may not be entered into AI tools clearly defined?
Q28.strengthText: Rules for input into AI tools are clearly defined

Q29.text: Is there a rule requiring human review of AI-generated code?
Q29.strengthText: Human review of AI-generated code is established as a rule

Q30.text: Do you have guidelines or instruction files for AI to reference?
Q30.strengthText: Guidelines and instruction files for AI reference are in place

Q31.text: Do you have metrics to measure the effectiveness of AI usage?
Q31.strengthText: Metrics exist for measuring the effectiveness of AI usage

Q32.text: Is there a person responsible for or driving AI adoption?
Q32.strengthText: A structure for driving AI adoption is in place

Q34.text: Are AI tools able to securely reference internal data such as internal documents, the codebase, and knowledge bases?
Q34.strengthText: An environment where AI can securely reference internal data is in place
Q34.description: This refers to a state where, through measures such as maintaining documentation within the repository and integrating tools, AI can reference internal data in compliance with rules for handling confidential information.

options.sufficient: Can reference most of the necessary internal data
options.not_implemented: Not yet in a referenceable state

imp-Q01.title: Develop an AI utilization plan suited to the development phase
imp-Q01.description: The scope and risk of AI utilization vary greatly depending on the type of development. In particular, for legacy renewal or the planning/consideration phase, establishing the foundation should come first.
imp-Q01.action: Clarify the development phase and create an AI utilization roadmap tailored to that phase.
imp-Q01.expectedEffect: Prevents unrealistic expectations and enables a gradual, reliable rollout of AI adoption.

imp-Q02.title: Understand the technical characteristics of the development domain
imp-Q02.description: In areas with limited track record for general AI coding assistance, such as embedded control and data analysis/ML, careful adoption is required.
imp-Q02.action: Consider AI tools and prompt designs specialized for the target domain.
imp-Q02.expectedEffect: Selecting tools that account for domain characteristics avoids mismatches.

imp-Q03.title: Consider migrating to a common technology stack
imp-Q03.description: AI can provide more accurate assistance for widely used technologies. The benefits of AI utilization are limited for proprietary or rare technologies.
imp-Q03.action: As a long-term technology selection policy, consider adopting a widely used technology stack.
imp-Q03.expectedEffect: Improves the accuracy and quality of suggestions from AI coding assistance.

imp-Q04.title: Eliminate reliance on specific individuals for understanding specifications
imp-Q04.description: If understanding of specifications depends on a specific person, the review and verification structure will not function properly when using AI.
imp-Q04.action: Carry out pair work and documentation to share understanding of specifications among multiple people.
imp-Q04.expectedEffect: Strengthens the verification structure for AI-generated output and improves suitability for the implementation process.

imp-Q05.title: Maintain the README and environment setup procedures
imp-Q05.description: A poorly maintained README is a major barrier to understanding the development context, both for new team members and for AI tools.
imp-Q05.action: Clearly document the project overview, setup procedure, how to run the application, and testing methods in the README, and verify them regularly.
imp-Q05.expectedEffect: Enables AI coding tools to reference the correct development context.

imp-Q06.title: Move specifications from chat to formal documents
imp-Q06.description: Having specifications scattered across chat and email, or barely retained at all, is a serious risk that directly caps the overall score.
imp-Q06.action: Consolidate requirements and specifications into a wiki or document management tool, and define an update workflow.
imp-Q06.expectedEffect: Enables AI to reference specifications during implementation, and removes the cap on the overall score.

imp-Q07.title: Introduce ADRs (Architecture Decision Records)
imp-Q07.description: If the background behind design decisions is not recorded, AI cannot make consistent proposals that take past decisions into account.
imp-Q07.action: First prepare a template, then start recording from future important design decisions onward.
imp-Q07.expectedEffect: Allows AI to understand design intent and reduces proposals that contradict past decisions.

imp-Q08.title: Establish coding guidelines
imp-Q08.description: Without established conventions, the style of AI-generated code becomes inconsistent, increasing review costs.
imp-Q08.action: Extract conventions from existing code and document them in a form AI can reference.
imp-Q08.expectedEffect: Improves consistency of AI-generated code and reduces review burden.

imp-Q09.title: Document API specifications and external integration specifications
imp-Q09.description: Without documented API specifications, there is a higher risk of AI generating code with incorrect interfaces.
imp-Q09.action: Document API specifications in a format such as OpenAPI, and introduce a mechanism to keep them in sync with the implementation.
imp-Q09.expectedEffect: Reduces the risk of AI generating incorrect code in implementations involving external integrations.

imp-Q10.title: Document database design and data fields
imp-Q10.description: Insufficient documentation of the data model becomes an obstacle to AI-assisted implementation that requires data consistency.
imp-Q10.action: Maintain ER diagrams and data field definitions, and establish rules for updating them when changes occur.
imp-Q10.expectedEffect: Reduces the risk of AI generating incorrect code in data-related implementations.

imp-Q11.title: Document business rules and exception handling
imp-Q11.description: Business rules that exist only as tacit knowledge cannot be understood by AI, and become a cause of serious specification omissions.
imp-Q11.action: Document business rules and exception patterns explicitly, either as documentation or as test cases.
imp-Q11.expectedEffect: Reduces rework when using AI during requirements organization and implementation.

imp-Q12.title: Turn past incidents and known issues into knowledge
imp-Q12.description: Without records of past incidents, there is a risk of repeating similar problems, and AI has nothing to reference either.
imp-Q12.action: Accumulate incident reports and precautions in a searchable location.
imp-Q12.expectedEffect: Improves suitability for AI utilization in operations and incident response processes.

imp-Q13.title: Migrate to version control with Git
imp-Q13.description: Not using Git makes tracking change history and reviewing diffs of AI-generated code extremely difficult, and is a direct cause of the cap on the overall score.
imp-Q13.action: Migrate all source code to a Git repository and establish basic branch operation rules.
imp-Q13.expectedEffect: Establishes the prerequisites for AI-driven development and removes the cap on the overall score.

imp-Q14.title: Introduce commit message conventions
imp-Q14.description: If the purpose of a change cannot be inferred from the commit history, neither humans nor AI can understand the intent of the change.
imp-Q14.action: Introduce a commit message convention that includes the purpose of the change and ticket numbers.
imp-Q14.expectedEffect: Improves the readability of change history and the accuracy with which AI understands diffs.

imp-Q15.title: Establish a Pull Request review process
imp-Q15.description: The absence of human review eliminates the opportunity to verify AI-generated code, and is a cause of the cap on the overall score.
imp-Q15.action: Introduce a Pull Request / Merge Request-based development flow and make review mandatory.
imp-Q15.expectedEffect: Enables AI-generated code to be incorporated safely, and removes the cap on the overall score.

imp-Q16.title: Introduce a ticket management tool
imp-Q16.description: Without ticket management, task granularity and progress become opaque, which is a cause of the cap on the overall score.
imp-Q16.action: Introduce a ticket management tool such as GitHub Issues or Jira, and establish its use as standard operating procedure.
imp-Q16.expectedEffect: Clarifies the task units that can be delegated to AI, and removes the cap on the overall score.

imp-Q17.title: Include the purpose, requirements, and completion criteria in tickets
imp-Q17.description: If completion criteria are ambiguous, the granularity of tasks delegated to AI cannot be determined, and the criteria for verifying deliverables also become unclear.
imp-Q17.action: Add purpose, requirements, and completion criteria as required fields in the ticket template.
imp-Q17.expectedEffect: Improves the accuracy of delegating tasks to AI, and helps push up the estimated reduction rate.

imp-Q18.title: Break down tasks into an appropriate granularity
imp-Q18.description: Tasks that are too coarse make instructions to AI ambiguous and increase the cost of verifying the generated code.
imp-Q18.action: Thoroughly implement the practice of breaking down tasks into units that can be completed in a few hours to a few days.
imp-Q18.expectedEffect: Increases the number of tasks that can be delegated to AI agents and reduces review burden.

imp-Q19.title: Standardize the procedures from development through release
imp-Q19.description: If procedures depend on specific individuals or are undefined, it is not possible to build a standard workflow that incorporates AI utilization.
imp-Q19.action: Create a procedure manual covering everything from development to release, and communicate it to the team.
imp-Q19.expectedEffect: Improves the reproducibility of the entire development workflow, including AI utilization.

imp-Q20.title: Build a CI/CD or automated build system
imp-Q20.description: Without automated builds and automated checks, the verification cycle for AI-generated code becomes prolonged.
imp-Q20.action: Build a pipeline that automates builds, tests, and static checks.
imp-Q20.expectedEffect: Enables AI-generated code to be verified quickly and safely, and helps push up the estimated reduction rate.

imp-Q33.title: Establish the practice of small commits and rollback operations
imp-Q33.description: AI utilization tends to increase the volume of changes, and without a practice of fine-grained commits and the ability to roll back, reverting changes becomes difficult when problems occur.
imp-Q33.action: Establish, as a team practice, committing work in small units and procedures for safely reverting using tools like revert.
imp-Q33.expectedEffect: Enables AI-driven changes to be incorporated safely and allows quick recovery when problems occur.

imp-Q21.title: Establish automated tests
imp-Q21.description: The absence of automated tests is a serious risk that removes the safety net for AI-generated code.
imp-Q21.action: Progressively build unit tests starting with important features, and incorporate them into CI.
imp-Q21.expectedEffect: Enables the quality of AI-generated code to be mechanically assured, and removes the cap on the overall score.

imp-Q22.title: Clarify the checks to perform after a change
imp-Q22.description: Without defined checks, it is not possible to verify the impact of changes (especially those made by AI).
imp-Q22.action: Prepare a verification checklist for each type of change.
imp-Q22.expectedEffect: Makes verification of AI-generated code reliable, and removes the cap on the overall score.

imp-Q23.title: Document code review standards
imp-Q23.description: If review standards depend on individual reviewers, quality judgments will vary, and evaluation of AI-generated code will also be inconsistent.
imp-Q23.action: Document review criteria such as security, readability, and performance, and turn them into a checklist.
imp-Q23.expectedEffect: Improves suitability for AI utilization in the code review process.

imp-Q24.title: Automate static analysis, linting, and type checking
imp-Q24.description: Without automated checks, the quality of AI-generated code cannot be mechanically assured.
imp-Q24.action: Incorporate linting, type checking, and static analysis tools into CI.
imp-Q24.expectedEffect: Enables automatic detection of obvious problems in AI-generated code and reduces review burden.

imp-Q25.title: Establish a human approval process before production deployment
imp-Q25.description: The lack of human approval undermines safety when using AI, and is a cause of the cap on the overall score.
imp-Q25.action: Build a workflow that always includes a human approval step before production deployment.
imp-Q25.expectedEffect: Enables safe releases even when using AI, and removes the cap on the overall score.

imp-Q26.title: Establish rules for handling confidential and personal information
imp-Q26.description: Without established handling rules, the risk of information leakage increases when using AI tools.
imp-Q26.action: Establish classification criteria and handling rules for confidential and personal information, and communicate them.
imp-Q26.expectedEffect: Reduces the risk of information leakage and establishes the prerequisites for defining AI input rules.

imp-Q27.title: Expand the adoption of AI coding tools
imp-Q27.description: If AI usage remains unimplemented or stuck at the trial stage, progress on measuring effectiveness and building a support structure will stall.
imp-Q27.action: Carry out a small-scale pilot rollout and record the effects and challenges.
imp-Q27.expectedEffect: Accumulates track-record data on AI utilization and provides a basis for deciding on full-scale rollout.

imp-Q28.title: Define information that must not be entered into AI
imp-Q28.description: Without rules defining what information may or may not be entered into AI, the risk of leaking confidential information is high, and this is a cause of the cap on the overall score.
imp-Q28.action: Establish criteria for whether information can be entered into AI tools for each confidentiality classification, and operate them together with tool settings.
imp-Q28.expectedEffect: Enables safe use of AI, and removes the cap on the overall score.

imp-Q29.title: Establish review rules for AI-generated code
imp-Q29.description: Without established review criteria specific to AI-generated code (such as incorrect assumptions or licensing issues), quality risks remain.
imp-Q29.action: Establish a review checklist specifically for AI-generated code.
imp-Q29.expectedEffect: Establishes a structure for incorporating AI-generated code with confidence.

imp-Q30.title: Prepare guidelines and instruction files for AI reference
imp-Q30.description: Without instruction files for AI tools, it becomes difficult to ensure consistency in AI-generated output.
imp-Q30.action: Document coding conventions and design policies as AI-reference files such as AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md.
imp-Q30.expectedEffect: Improves the consistency and accuracy of AI-generated code.

imp-Q31.title: Introduce metrics to measure the effectiveness of AI usage
imp-Q31.description: Without effectiveness metrics, return on investment cannot be explained, delaying decisions to expand AI utilization.
imp-Q31.action: Define KPIs such as implementation time, review time, and defect counts, and measure them continuously.
imp-Q31.expectedEffect: Provides a basis for decision-making on AI adoption and enables continuous improvement.

imp-Q32.title: Appoint a person to drive AI adoption
imp-Q32.description: Without someone driving adoption, establishing rules for AI utilization and its rollout becomes ad hoc.
imp-Q32.action: Appoint a person responsible for and driving AI adoption, and establish a structure for updating guidelines and selecting tools.
imp-Q32.expectedEffect: Enables AI utilization to continue and expand in an organized manner.

imp-Q34.title: Establish an environment where AI can reference internal data
imp-Q34.description: If AI tools cannot reference specifications, the codebase, and knowledge, the quality of generated output will hit a ceiling due to insufficient context.
imp-Q34.action: In compliance with rules for handling confidential information, establish a state where AI can reference internal data through measures such as maintaining documentation within the repository and integrating tools.
imp-Q34.expectedEffect: Improves the accuracy and consistency of AI output and reduces rework.
